pattern: Sort the pairs by their right endpoint and greedily extend the chain whenever the next pair starts strictly after the last chosen pair's end.
hints: Order is free, so reorder the pairs to make a greedy choice possible.
Sorting by the second value of each pair lets you always pick the pair that frees up the earliest, like interval scheduling.
Walk the sorted pairs, taking a pair only when its left value exceeds the last taken right value, and count how many you take.

edge_inputs.description: overlapping middle pair limits the chain to two
disjoint pairs given out of order chain to three
single pair is a chain of length one
touching endpoints b equals c do not chain
fully nested pairs allow only one in the chain
greedy by end beats greedy by start
extreme value bounds in one pair

stress.description: many random valid pairs with left strictly less than right
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    pairs = []
    for _ in range(size):
        a = rng.randint(-1000, 999)
        b = rng.randint(a + 1, 1000)
        pairs.append([a, b])
    return [pairs]
